notes: VS Code MCP uses 'servers' key (NOT 'mcpServers') — different from Claude/Cursor
VS Code MCP requires explicit 'type' field on all entries
VS Code MCP supports 'inputs' array for user-prompted values
Coding Agent MCP is configured in GitHub repo settings (web UI), not a file
Coding Agent MCP requires 'tools' field (mandatory as of Mar 2026)
Coding Agent MCP supports COPILOT_MCP_<NAME> variable substitution for secrets
hooks.json requires 'version: 1' at top level
Hook entries use 'bash' field (not 'command'), 'timeoutSec' (not 'timeout' in ms)
Only 'command' hook type supported (no 'prompt' or 'agent')
Hook events are camelCase (preToolUse, not PreToolUse)
Only 6 hook events supported as of Mar 2026: sessionStart, sessionEnd, userPromptSubmitted, preToolUse, postToolUse, errorOccurred (agentStop and subagentStop removed)
Agent files use .agent.md extension
Agent frontmatter now supports 'name' and 'user-invocable' fields (Mar 2026)
Agent 'infer' field retired (Mar 2026)
Agent tools use aliases (read, edit, execute, search, agent, web)
Skills output to .github/skills/<name>/SKILL.md; .claude/skills is also supported by Copilot as of Mar 2026
Skills only support name, description, license — all other fields are over-emitting
Rules: excludeAgent frontmatter ('code-review' | 'coding-agent')
No file-based permissions — CLI flags only
No file-based ignore — web UI content exclusion only
Copilot evolves rapidly — check docs monthly
Detailed gap analysis in .ai/research/copilot-capabilities.md
